name: Gudrun
description: Gudrun, also known as Kriemhild in the Nibelungenlied, is a prominent figure in Norse heroic legend. She is a princess of the Burgundians, renowned for her beauty and strong will, but her life is marked by immense tragedy and vengeance. Gudrun's story is one of love, loss, betrayal, and relentless pursuit of justice. She is first married to Sigurd (Siegfried), the dragon-slayer, and bears him a son. However, through the deceit and treachery of Brynhild and the Gibichungs, Sigurd is murdered. Gudrun is then forced to marry Atli (Attila the Hun), a cruel and powerful king. Driven by grief and a desire for revenge against those who wronged her and her beloved Sigurd, Gudrun orchestrates the deaths of Atli and their children. Her story explores themes of fate, revenge, and the destructive power of betrayal, highlighting the strength and resilience of women in the face of unimaginable suffering. She is a complex character, both victim and perpetrator, a testament to the harsh realities of the heroic age. Later in life, she marries Jonakr and has three sons with him, further intertwining her fate with the cycles of violence and retribution that define much of Norse mythology. Gudrun's tale serves as a cautionary example of the devastating consequences of unchecked ambition and the enduring power of grief and vengeance.
subtitle: Vengeance incarnate, a queen of tragedy.
achievements: Survived the murder of her first husband
Orchestrated the deaths of Atli and their children
Remarried and bore more children after immense loss

keyMyths.title: The Murder of Sigurd
keyMyths.description: Sigurd, Gudrun's first husband, is murdered through the treachery of Brynhild and the Gibichungs. This event sets in motion Gudrun's quest for vengeance and shapes the rest of her life.

keyMyths.title: Gudrun's Marriage to Atli
keyMyths.description: Forced to marry Atli, Gudrun plots her revenge against him for his role in the deaths of her brothers. She ultimately kills him and their children in a gruesome act of retribution.

keyMyths.title: The Fall of the Nibelungs
keyMyths.description: Gudrun's actions contribute to the overall downfall of the Burgundian kingdom. The cycle of violence and betrayal consumes her family and leads to widespread destruction.
keyMyths.source: Völsunga saga and the Poetic Edda
